rationale: The signals show broad, evergreen searches dominating (care, diet, enclosures—all covered), but three concrete pockets of demand emerge: (1) rising heat/temperature confusion (+100%, +50%), (2) specific morph ID/genetics confusion (Cappuccino, Lilly White, Axanthic appearing across all channels with breeding-related friction), and (3) rare/niche morphs generating comment & debate. The Google Trends spike on "albino crested gecko" (+60%) is a red flag—no albino exists in captive geckos, so this is a misconception worth tackling. Cappuccino appears overrepresented in YouTube with conflicting titles ("scam" vs "opportunity"), suggesting breeder confusion. I'm skipping general care, listicles, and dupes. The heat lamp confusion and "albino" myth both have clear corrective angles and grounding in genetics.
